metric_name: Dirty secret: 40% don't file, 20% bungle (60% total breakage)
metric_value: The dirty little secret of the industry has been that 40% of consumers don't file the rebate and that 20% bungle the paperwork
notes: Kastner canonical breakage statistic; most-cited version

metric_name: Short mail-in deadlines (sometimes 14 days) are increasing
metric_value: The shadier ones are actually making it increasingly harder for shoppers to redeem their rebate offers, says Edgar Dworsky... A common ploy? Ever shortening mail-in deadlines. Sometimes you'll have just 14 days from time of purchase.

metric_name: FTC goes after companies for late/missing rebates
metric_value: Outright fraud is less common nowadays for mail-in rebates... the FTC goes after companies that don't send out rebates in a timely manner or fail to deliver on them at all

metric_name: Complain loudly, escalate to manufacturer/retailer, then BBB/FTC
metric_value: If you have a problem, complain and complain loud... call the customer service lines of the manufacturer and of the retailer. They will want to preserve that customer relationship.
Staples' Easy Rebates — most rebates can be activated by just entering two numbers from your store receipt on an online form

metric_name: Keep copies of all paperwork for resubmission
metric_value: Be sure to keep a copy of all the paperwork. That way, should your original get lost or rejected, you can most likely re-submit

metric_name: Staples Easy Rebates fully online by 2008
metric_value: Staples Easy Rebates became fully online submission by 2008, eliminating mail-in requirement entirely
SmartMoney magazine ceased print publication Sep 2012; content merged into WSJ personal-finance online; smartmoney.com URL redirected to WSJ
notes: Publisher context — article URL now dead

metric_name: Mail-in rebates largely extinct as US consumer category
metric_value: Mail-in rebates collapsed from $10B annually (2002) to <$500M by early 2020s per industry trackers; replaced by instant rebates, cashback cards, gift cards, and online-activation codes
notes: Validates Kastner 2006 framing of rebate-industry unsustainability
